item.title: 【Advanced Workday Reporting in Practice: From Calculated Fields to Prism Analytics】高级工作日报告实践：从计算字段到 Prism 分析
item.description:
【

In the Workday ecosystem, reporting capabilities range from simple built-in reports to highly customized analytics that blend external data. For an intermediate-level audience, mastering advanced reporting means leveraging calculated fields within Workday’s report writer and pushing into Workday Prism Analytics for even deeper insights. 

This article delves into technical concepts behind these tools, showing how calculated fields serve as building blocks for complex reports, and how Prism Analytics expands reporting beyond Workday’s core data. Along the way, we’ll include illustrative code snippets and diagrams to clarify these concepts in practice.

】

在 Workday 生态系统中，报告功能范围从简单的内置报告到混合外部数据的高度定制的分析。对于中级受众来说，掌握高级报告意味着利用 Workday 报告编写器中的计算字段并推进 Workday Prism Analytics 以获得更深入的见解。 

本文深入探讨了这些工具背后的技术概念，展示了计算字段如何作为复杂报告的构建块，以及 Prism Analytics 如何将报告扩展到 Workday 核心数据之外。在此过程中，我们将提供说明性代码片段和图表，以在实践中阐明这些概念。

item.title: 【Google Cloud AI Agents With Gemini 3: Building Multi-Agent Systems That Actually Work】Google Cloud AI 代理与 Gemini 3：构建实际工作的多代理系统
item.description:
【

The transition from large language models (LLMs) as simple chat interfaces to autonomous AI agents represents the most significant shift in enterprise software since the move to microservices. With the release of Gemini 3, Google Cloud has provided the foundational model capable of long-context reasoning and low-latency decision-making required for sophisticated multi-agent systems (MAS).

However, building an agent that "actually works" — one that is reliable, observable, and capable of handling edge cases — requires more than a prompt and an API key. It requires a robust architectural framework, a deep understanding of tool use, and a structured approach to agent orchestration.

】

从作为简单聊天界面的大型语言模型 (LLM) 到自主 AI 代理的转变代表了企业软件自转向微服务以来最重大的转变。随着 Gemini 3 的发布，Google Cloud 提供了能够实现复杂多代理系统 (MAS) 所需的长上下文推理和低延迟决策的基础模型。

但是，构建一个“真正有效”的代理（可靠、可观察且能够处理边缘情况）需要的不仅仅是提示和 API 密钥。它需要强大的架构框架、对工具使用的深刻理解以及代理编排的结构化方法。

item.title: 【Beyond the Heartbeat: Monitoring Agentic Systems】超越心跳：监控代理系统
item.description:
【

The key aspect of monitoring an agentic system is that you’re no longer monitoring a predictable web server responding to deterministic requests in a predefined way. You’re observing a semi-autonomous reasoning system that thinks and decides using the instructions, context, and tools at its disposal.

So, What’s Different?

An agentic platform is a distributed decision engine with its own working memory and evolving context. It makes choices, adapts, and improvises — at times in ways you did not explicitly script.

】

监控代理系统的关键在于您不再监控以预定义方式响应确定性请求的可预测 Web 服务器。您正在观察一个半自主推理系统，它使用可用的指令、上下文和工具进行思考和决策。

那么，有什么不同？

代理平台是一个分布式决策引擎，拥有自己的工作内存和不断变化的上下文。它会做出选择、适应和即兴发挥——有时是以您没有明确编写脚本的方式。

item.title: 【What's New in Java 25: Key Changes From Java 21】Java 25 的新增功能：相对于 Java 21 的主要变化
item.description:
【

On the 16th of September 2025, Java 25 was released. Time to take a closer look at the changes since the last LTS release, which is Java 21. In this blog, some of the changes between Java 21 and Java 25 are highlighted, mainly by means of examples. Enjoy!

Introduction

What has changed between Java 21 and Java 25? A complete list of the JEPs (Java Enhancement Proposals) can be found at the OpenJDK website. Here you can read the nitty-gritty details of each JEP. For a complete list of what has changed per release since Java 21, the Oracle release notes give a good overview.

】

2025 年 9 月 16 日，Java 25 发布。是时候仔细看看自上一个 LTS 版本（Java 21）以来的变化了。在本博客中，主要通过示例的方式重点介绍了 Java 21 和 Java 25 之间的一些变化。享受吧！

简介

Java 21 和 Java 25 之间发生了哪些变化？ JEP（Java 增强提案）的完整列表可以在 OpenJDK 网站上找到。您可以在此处阅读每个 JEP 的详细信息。有关自 Java 21 以来每个版本更改内容的完整列表，Oracle 发行说明提供了很好的概述。

item.title: 【The Global Race to Govern AI Agents Has Begun】治理人工智能代理的全球竞赛已经开始
item.description:
【

In late January 2026, a startup CEO launched a Reddit-style social network called Moltbook — exclusively for AI agents. Within days, it claimed 1.5 million autonomous agents posting, commenting, and upvoting[1]. OpenAI founding member Andrej Karpathy initially called it “the most incredible sci-fi takeoff-adjacent thing I’ve seen recently.” Then security researchers at Wiz found an exposed database API key on the front end of the site — granting full read and write access to the entire production database, including 1.5 million API authentication tokens and 35,000 email addresses[2]. Karpathy reversed course: “It’s a dumpster fire. I definitely do not recommend people run this stuff on their computers.”

Moltbook is not an edge case. It is a preview of what happens when autonomous AI agents operate without governance. And the timing is striking: the very same week Moltbook went viral, Singapore’s Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) released the world’s first governance framework built specifically for agentic AI[3]. One event showed the fire. The other offered the fire code.

】

2026 年 1 月下旬，一位初创公司首席执行官推出了一个名为 Moltbook 的 Reddit 风格社交网络 - 专门针对人工智能代理。几天之内，就有 150 万自主代理发帖、评论和点赞[1]。 OpenAI 创始成员 Andrej Karpathy 最初称其为“我最近见过的最令人难以置信的科幻起飞相​​关事物”。随后，Wiz 的安全研究人员在网站前端发现了一个暴露的数据库 API 密钥，授予对整个生产数据库的完全读写访问权限，其中包括 150 万个 API 身份验证令牌和 35,000 个电子邮件地址[2]。卡帕蒂扭转了路线：“这是一场垃圾箱火灾。我绝对不建议人们在计算机上运行这些东西。”

Moltbook 并不是一个极端情况。这是当自主人工智能代理在没有治理的情况下运行时会发生什么的预览。时机非常惊人：就在 Moltbook 火爆的同一周，新加坡信息通信媒体发展局 (IMDA) 发布了世界上第一个专门为代理 AI 构建的治理框架[3]。一场事件显示了火灾。另一个提供了消防代码。

item.title: 【Escaping the “Demo Trap”: A Guide to Engineering Reliable AI Agents】逃离“演示陷阱”：设计可靠的人工智能代理指南
item.description:
【

We are currently witnessing a paradox in the AI industry. On one hand, building a generative AI demo has never been easier; with a few lines of Python and an API key, a developer can spin up a "chat with your data" prototype in an afternoon. On the other hand, deploying a reliable, autonomous agent into a production environment remains notoriously difficult. The chasm between these two states is the "Demo Trap."

Image Source: Robert Lukeman on Unsplash (For Illustrative purposes only)

】

我们目前正在目睹人工智能行业的一个悖论。一方面，构建生成式 AI 演示从未如此简单；只需几行 Python 代码和一个 API 密钥，开发人员就可以在一个下午内启动“与数据聊天”原型。另一方面，在生产环境中部署可靠的自主代理仍然非常困难。这两种状态之间的鸿沟就是“演示陷阱”。

图片来源：Robert Lukeman on Unsplash（仅用于说明目的）

item.title: 【DPoP: What It Is, How It Works, and Why Bearer Tokens Aren't Enough】DPoP：它是什么、如何运作以及为什么不记名代币还不够
item.description:
【

DPoP is one of the most exciting developments in the identity and access management (IAM) space in recent years. Yet many backend developers either have not heard of it or are unsure what it actually changes. In this article, I will break down what DPoP is, what problem it solves, and walk through a working implementation with Keycloak and Quarkus.

What Is DPoP?

Demonstration of Proof-of-Possession (DPoP) is an OAuth 2.0 security mechanism defined in RFC 9449. Its core purpose is simple: cryptographically bind an access token to the client that requested it. This way, even if a token is intercepted, it cannot be used by another client.

】

DPoP 是近年来身份和访问管理 (IAM) 领域最令人兴奋的发展之一。然而许多后端开发人员要么没有听说过它，要么不确定它实际改变了什么。在本文中，我将详细介绍 DPoP 是什么、它解决什么问题，并介绍使用 Keycloak 和 Quarkus 的有效实现。

什么是 DPoP？

所有权证明演示 (DPoP) 是 RFC 中定义的 OAuth 2.0 安全机制9449。 其核心目的很简单：以加密方式将访问令牌绑定到请求它的客户端。 这样，即使令牌被拦截，其他客户端也无法使用它。

item.title: 【The Human Bottleneck in DevOps Automating Knowledge with AIOps and SECI】DevOps 中的人为瓶颈通过 AIOps 和 SECI 实现知识自动化
item.description:
【

In modern IT operations (ITOps), we face a paradox: our infrastructure is dynamic, scalable, and cloud-native, but our operational processes are often static, manual, and dependent on a few hero engineers.

When an incident occurs, the mean time to recovery (MTTR) often depends less on the technology stack and more on who is on call. If the expert is unavailable, the system stays down. This is the knowledge bottleneck.

】

在现代 IT 运营 (ITOps) 中，我们面临一个悖论：我们的基础设施是动态的、可扩展的、云原生的，但我们的运营流程通常是静态的、手动的，并且依赖于少数英雄工程师。

发生事件时，平均恢复时间 (MTTR) 通常较少取决于技术堆栈，而更多取决于待命人员。如果专家不在，系统就会停止运行。这是知识瓶颈。

item.title: 【Cost Control in AI Systems Is an Architectural Problem】人工智能系统中的成本控制是一个架构问题
item.description:
【

For any working system, whether AI or non-AI, operating costs play a significant role throughout the product lifecycle. In the case of AI systems, these costs are calculated by estimating future usage and concurrency. Usually, these cost estimates determine the pricing that end users are expected to pay. But the critical problem arises when these estimates turn out to be way off from the actuals. In that case, operating costs rise significantly, hurting margins, and the business suffers overall.

When such things happen, people assume it's because of the expensive model, which is not true. These systems become expensive because their architectures multiply costs.

】

对于任何工作系统，无论是人工智能还是非人工智能，运营成本在整个产品生命周期中都发挥着重要作用。就人工智能系统而言，这些成本是通过估计未来的使用情况和并发性来计算的。通常，这些成本估算决定了最终用户预计支付的定价。但当这些估计结果与实际情况相差甚远时，关键问题就出现了。在这种情况下，运营成本会大幅上升，从而损害利润，整个业务都会受到影响。

当此类事情发生时，人们会认为这是因为型号昂贵，但事实并非如此。这些系统变得昂贵，因为它们的架构增加了成本。

item.title: 【Essential Techniques for Production Vector Search Systems, Part 4: Multi-Vector Search】生产向量搜索系统的基本技术，第 4 部分：多向量搜索
item.description:
【

After implementing vector search systems at multiple companies, I wanted to document efficient techniques that can be very helpful for successful production deployments of vector search systems.

I want to present these techniques by showcasing when to apply each one, how they complement each other, and the trade-offs they introduce. This will be a multi-part series that introduces all of the techniques one by one in each article. I have also included code snippets to quickly test each technique.

】

在多家公司实施矢量搜索系统后，我想记录一些有效的技术，这些技术对于矢量搜索系统的成功生产部署非常有帮助。

我想通过展示何时应用每种技术、它们如何相互补充以及它们引入的权衡来展示这些技术。这将是一个由多部分组成的系列，每篇文章都会一一介绍所有技术。我还提供了代码片段来快速测试每种技术。
